rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior conjoint experiment-design work and discusses limits of earlier balancing methods that rely on prior partworth knowledge. The cited paper is directly about Bayesian conjoint choice design efficiency using managers’ prior beliefs (DB/DP-error framework), which is a plausible methodological/foundational antecedent for the citing paper’s design-focused argument. Given this strong topical and methodological alignment (despite only DOI-level match evidence), this is not safely an obvious mention-only citation.
